name: Functional Bulk Dynamic
description: Multi-week hybrid program for getting functionally large. 3 heavy gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) for size and strength, 2 kettlebell days for conditioning, plus an optional Zone 2 day. Unlike the standard Functional Bulk, this version uses fixed exercises each week (no randomization) so you can track progression, and evolves week-to-week with different exercises and rep schemes.
creator: Custom

weeks.label: Foundation
weeks.description: Establish your baseline. Learn the movement patterns, find your working weights, and build the habit. All exercises are fixed so you know exactly what you're doing each session.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Core, mobility, bodyweight legs. ~30 min on a mat.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Hip-heavy mobility + deep stretches. Everything is sore — move through it.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min hip primer. No leg work — just open up for squats.

tips: Focus on learning the movement patterns — form over weight
Find your working weights: the last 2 reps of each set should be challenging but doable
Write down your weights so you can beat them next week
If you can complete all sets at the top of the rep range, increase weight next session

weeks.label: Accumulate
weeks.description: You know your working weights. Now beat them. Same core lifts with small accessory swaps to keep it fresh. Your body is adapting — you should feel less wrecked than Week 1.

schedule.label: PUSH

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min shoulder/chest opener. Band pull-aparts, arm circles, easy push-ups.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5 (easy, don't go to failure). Pull-ups don't interfere with push day.

schedule.label: KB FULL BODY

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same flow as W1 — core, mobility, bodyweight legs. ~30 min on a mat.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Tomorrow is Pull day — keep these easy.

schedule.label: PULL

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min back/lat primer. Dead hangs, scap pulls, band pull-aparts.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Skip home pull-ups — you're doing them at the gym today.

schedule.label: KB CONDITIONING

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same hip-heavy flow as W1. You're sore but less sore than last week. Move through it.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Back recovering from Pull day yesterday.

schedule.label: LEGS

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: Same 15 min hip primer. Don't skip it this week — your squats will thank you.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Legs and pull-ups don't compete.

schedule.label: ZONE 2 / OPTIONAL
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5 if you feel good. Easy day.

schedule.label: REST
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s. That's it — rest day.

tips: You know your working weights — hit them from set 1, no ramping up
Goal: add 1-2 reps per exercise compared to W1, or add 5lbs where you topped the rep range
New exercises this week: pec deck, overhead tricep extension, DB rows, step-ups, KB high pulls, Turkish get-ups
Turkish get-ups: go LIGHT. This is a skill you're learning, not a strength test.
Try to do the morning flows this week — W1 you skipped most of them

weeks.label: Intensify
weeks.description: You know your body now. Weights go up where W2 was too easy (leg curls, calf raises, cable lat raises). New movements: push press on push day, preacher curl on pull day, bent press + cossack squat on KB day. Tuesday Explore / Thursday Refine is locked in.

schedule.label: PUSH

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min warrior flow + scorpion stretch + hollow body. More intense than W1-W2.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8 (push for 8 this week). Pull-ups don't interfere with push day.

schedule.label: KB FULL BODY (EXPLORE)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min harder core (hollow rocks, plank shoulder taps) + KB-specific hip/thoracic mobility for bent press and cossack squat.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5. Tomorrow is Pull day — keep these easy.

schedule.label: PULL

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min back primer + deeper hip openers (frog stretch, happy baby). Upgraded from W2.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Skip home pull-ups — you're doing them at the gym today.

schedule.label: KB CONDITIONING (REFINE)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min recovery-focused. Plow pose, pigeon, frog stretch, light core. If time-crunched, skip core block.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s only. Back recovering from Pull day yesterday.

schedule.label: LEGS

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min dynamic squat prep. Warrior flow, skandasana, crow pose attempt. Primes heavy squats.

schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8. Legs and pull-ups don't compete.

schedule.label: ZONE 2 / OPTIONAL
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s + pull-ups 2-3x5-8 if you feel good. Easy day.

options.label: GTC Floor Workout
options.notes: Boredom-busting floor workout: barre burn, plyo sweat, push-up party, core destroyer. ~25 min, no equipment. Do all 4 blocks or cherry-pick.

activity.name: Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Get outside. Conversational pace. Last week's hikes were perfect Zone 2.

schedule.label: REST
schedule.homeGym: Dead hangs 2-3x20s. That's it — rest day.

tips: Weights go up this week where W2 was too easy: leg curls (90), calf raises (450), cable lat raises (20)
Push press replaces strict shoulder press — use leg drive to go heavier overhead
Preacher curl replaces hammer curl — way harder, note which EZ bar weight is your working weight
KB Explore day: bent press and cossack squat are new. Go light, learn the patterns.
KB Refine day: drill Tuesday's new stuff + heavier Simba lift and overhead lunges
WRITE DOWN which lat pulldown and cable row machine you use at Arrillaga
DB rows are now a planned pyramid — stop pretending it was accidental

weeks.label: GTC Week
weeks.description: GTC week — schedule shifted mid-week. Wed became a rest day (9am-7pm demos). Pull moved to Thu, Legs Fri, KB Sat optional. Fresh legs for squats again — no KB the day before.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min. Thread-the-needle + wall angels + puppy pose. Different from W3.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hip flow. Thread-the-needle + cossack bodyweight.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 12 min. Scapular pull-ups + dead hang + supine twists. Back primer.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: NEW — 15 min. Pigeon pose + glute bridge. Legs are FRESH — rest day yesterday, no KB day before.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Light and easy end-of-week.

tips: GTC week: schedule shifted — Wed rest, Thu pull, Fri legs, Sat KB optional.
Bench: no warmup sets at 55 — 65/side is your working weight now. Start there.
Legs on Friday with FRESH legs — no KB the day before for the first time. Use that.
KB Explore Tuesday: bent press 30lb, windmill 25lb.
KB Refine Saturday: finisher is 2 rounds only — legs day was yesterday.
DB row top set: 75lb PR attempt. Brace hard.

weeks.label: Shuffle Week
weeks.description: Same exercises, reordered to get honest numbers. Push press moves to station 1 (before chest fatigue) — does 50/side go overhead with fresh shoulders? Preacher curl moves right after pull-ups (before cable curls kill biceps). Legs stay Friday. Thursday KB Refine is genuinely light — skill drilling only, no leg loading. If meetings push Thursday late, swap Thu KB / Fri Legs freely.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — overhead mobility priority. Pike push-ups new. Push press is FIRST today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hip opener. Scorpion stretch new. Primes bent press and flow work.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep added since preacher curl is early today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Light and easy, legs are tomorrow.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — lizard pose new. Plank hip dips replace hollow body. 70/side is the floor today.

tips: SHUFFLE WEEK: same exercises, reordered. Push press first on push day, preacher curl early on pull day.
Push press with fresh shoulders: does 50/side finally go overhead? This is the test.
Preacher curl after pull-ups only: find your real working weight without cable curl fatigue.
Thursday KB is LIGHT — 20 min skill work only. No swings, no C&P, no squats. Legs are tomorrow.
Thursday/Friday are swappable — if meetings run late, do legs Thu and KB Fri.
Squat at 70/side for all working sets. Try 75 on the last set if it's clean.
Calf raises: 7 plates. Yes, really.

weeks.label: Lock In
weeks.description: Shuffle order is permanent — push press stays first, preacher stays early. W5 proved the reorder works: 50/side x8 overhead, 15/side preacher honest. Now lock in and progress. Chest press moves to station 2 (fresher). Squat 75/side attempt. DB row 80lb PR. Calves trying 7 plates. KB: full Explore/Refine split, Thursday stays light.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — band external rotations added for shoulder health. Overhead mobility priority.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hips. Same as W5 but deeper.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep. Dead hang 25s, scap pull-ups x10.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Be boring.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — 75/side attempt today. Hip mobility matters more than ever.

tips: LOCK IN: shuffle order is permanent. Push press first, preacher early, chest press station 2.
Push press 50/side: 3x8 clean is the target. W5 was 8/8/7.
Chest press at station 2 should be ~160-170 (was 175 first, 140 last).
Pull-ups: CLEAN SETS ONLY. No more fragmenting. 30 clean > 34 messy.
DB row 80lb top set — your back is ready.
Squat 75/side on the last set. Even 5 reps is a win.
Calf raises: 7 plates. Single-leg if you max the machine.
Thursday KB: boring. Legs tomorrow. You know the drill.

weeks.label: Form Check
weeks.description: Same exercises, form focus. Weights stay flat — stop sets when form breaks, don't chase reps. New exercises: Kinesis cable fly (pec deck broken), chest supported DB row, high cable bicep curl, cable crunch. B-Stance flow trimmed to 3x3 (was 5x5 = way too long). RDL is the form check exercise of the week — nail the bracing.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — shoulder rehab kept. Overhead mobility. Push press form is the focus today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hips. B-Stance flow is trimmed today.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep. Two new exercises today: high cable curl + cable crunch.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same flow as Tuesday. Be boring. Legs tomorrow.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — hip depth + glute activation. RDL bracing is the form check exercise today.

tips: FORM CHECK WEEK: stop sets when form breaks. Don't chase reps or weight.
Push press: parallel feet, no stagger. Accept 6 clean reps over 8 messy ones.
Kinesis cable fly: first time — scout the working number (1-12 scale).
Chest supported DB row: new. 40-50lb, strict. The preacher curl of rows.
High cable bicep curl + cable crunch: both new, both on cable column. Find working weights.
RDL is THE form check exercise — slow eccentric, brace before every rep, no back compensation.
Squat 75 baseline, 80 attempt on last set if clean.
B-Stance flow: 3 reps × 3 rounds (NOT 5x5). Learn from W6.
Thursday KB: boring. You know the drill.

weeks.label: Selective Push
weeks.description: Form check discoveries stay — push press at 45 parallel, incline DB at 50 with pause, leg press at 4 plates full ROM, back extensions at 25 slow. Push only on clean stuff: squat pyramids to 80 faster, chest press tries 185, DB rows try 85, cable crunch explores 120. Pull day trimmed (cable row dropped for chest supported). Usable strong > numbers.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — bodyweight shoulder work (no bands at home). Side-lying rotation, prone Y, scap push-ups.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hips. Same as W7.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep. Dead hang 25s, scap pull-ups x10.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same as Tuesday. Boring. Legs tomorrow.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — hip depth + glute activation. Squat pyramids to 80 faster today.

tips: SELECTIVE PUSH: form check stays on discoveries, push only on clean stuff.
Push press stays at 45 parallel. Don't rush back to 50.
Squat: pyramid to 80 faster — 75x8, 75x8, 80x8, 80x8.
Chest press: 180 is locked in. Try 185 on one set if 180 feels easy.
DB rows: 80x10 was clean. Try 85 on the top set.
Pull day is trimmed — cable row dropped, finisher cut to 2 sets each.
Leg press stays at 4 plates full ROM. Don't creep back to 6.
Back extensions at 25 slow. Hamstrings are just waking up.
Usable strong > numbers.

weeks.label: Recovery Deload
weeks.description: QL spasm recovery week. W7 form-check + W8 selective-push both drifted into PR territory on clean lifts, and W8D5 lunges triggered a bilateral QL spasm that lasted the weekend. W9 is a true deload: habit preserved, load cut 25-40%, QL offenders removed. Push press → seated DB press. No back extensions, no DB lunges, no Smith Bulgarians, no KB swings, no B-stance flow, no cable crunch. Daily QL release work. W10 returns to W8 loads IF recovery holds.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — adds pec minor release block (doorframe chest stretch). Targets the right-shoulder-blade → front-pec connection felt on W9D1 chest press.

schedule.label: KB LIGHT (skill only, no hip hinge)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — thoracic + hips. Keep it gentle this week.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 12 min — bicep/elbow prep. Pull-ups are decompressing this week, good for the back.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 10 min — same as Tuesday. Or skip entirely if back is still twitchy — take a walk instead.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: 15 min — QL/hip flexor release focus. Pigeon 90s each side. Longer holds than usual.

activity.name: Easy Walk
activity.notes: Best recovery cardio. Long flat walk, conversational pace.

activity.name: Flat Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Flat trails only this week — no steep descents that load the QL.

tips: RECOVERY WEEK — QL spasm over weekend after W8D5. Habit over intensity.
All working weights 60-80% of W8. Stop every set 2 reps shy of failure.
SKIPPED this week: back extensions, DB lunges, Smith Bulgarians, KB swings, B-stance flow, cable crunch, push press.
Push press → SEATED DB press. The standing overhead brace is what loads the QL.
Pull-ups are actually good this week — spinal decompression.
Legs day is the lightest of the program on purpose. Squat at 55/side is maintenance, not a workout.
Daily: lacrosse ball on QL, pigeon, child's pose, supine spinal twist.
If the back twinges at any point, stop the session. The recovery is the point.
W10 target: if recovery holds, ease back to W8 loads. Don't make up lost progression.

weeks.label: Form-First Return
weeks.description: Return from W9 deload, but NOT a 'pick up where W8 left off' week. This is a form-first cycle: weights pulled back ~10-15% from W8 highs across the board, modern 2026 form cues drilled on every lift, spotlight rotation kicks in (1-2 'spotlight' lifts per day get deep cue treatment, others get sharp cues only). Goal: build the base for years of clean, injury-free training. KBs return after W9 sick-week rest. Lunges way back (40 → 25) — QL respect. Spotlights this week: push press + incline DB (Mon), pull-up + DB row (Wed), squat + back extension (Fri). See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/form-cues-2026.md` for the full per-exercise cue reference.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W10 flow** — 15 min bodyweight + 3-5 min day-specific add-on at the end. **Today: do the PUSH add-on** — doorframe pec-minor stretch (LOW/MID/HIGH), prone Y raises, scap push-ups. Doorframe stretch is the KEY W9 protocol move; desk work re-tightens pec minor daily.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W10 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on at the end** — the in-workout banded prep block on Tue covers the equivalent.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W10 flow.** **Today: do the PULL add-on** — dead hang 25s + scapular pulls x10. Do at the gym before pull-ups if no bar at home. Scap engagement before loaded pulls.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W10 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on at the end** — the in-workout banded prep block on Thu covers the equivalent.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W10 flow.** **Today: do the LEGS add-on** — 90s pigeon (each side) + supine spinal twist. The W8 QL-spasm protocol; non-negotiable on legs day. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above iliac crest beforehand if available.

activity.name: Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Flat or rolling trails. If QL is fully quiet, can add modest descent — but pull back if anything talks.

tips: FORM-FIRST WEEK — every weight intentionally below W8. Modern 2026 cues drilled on every lift.
Spotlight rotation is the new structure: 1-2 spotlight lifts per day get deep cue treatment, others get sharp cues only.
Push: push press + incline DB. Pull: pull-up + DB row. Legs: squat + back extension.
See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/form-cues-2026.md` for the full reusable per-exercise cue reference.
KBs return after W9 sick-week rest — treat first sessions back as re-acquaintance, no PRs.
Lunges deloaded to 25lb (way back from W8's 40) — they're the QL spasm origin lift, treated with respect.
If a cue can't be held, the cue wins — drop weight, slow tempo, or skip the rep. No grinding bad reps.
QL is still the watchpoint. Abort any lift that twinges. Recovery confirmation > load progression.

weeks.label: Form-First Week 2 — Spotlights Rotate
weeks.description: Second week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate per the tracker: chest press machine (push), chest-supported DB row + lat pulldown (pull), leg press (legs), swing mechanics + bent press leverage (KB Tue), windmill mechanics (KB Thu). Big W10 takeaways baked in — squat brace pattern is locking in (hold 65), seated leg curl HARD RESET to 50 with corrected ankle placement (W10D5 form discovery — pad UNDER ankles, not on the calf), calf raise tempo strategy shift (double-leg slow OR single-leg faster, not both), C&P ramps 40 → 45 per W10D2 projection, lunges hold 25 (no QL spasm last week — earn the way back to 30 over multiple clean weeks), pull workout is now station-focused per the W10D3 meta-feedback (less machine-hopping). See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/form-cues-2026.md` for the full per-exercise cue reference.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W11 flow** (variety on W10's shape) — 15 min bodyweight + 3-5 min day-specific add-on. **Today: do the PUSH add-on** — doorframe pec-minor stretch (LOW/MID/HIGH), prone Y raises, scap push-ups. Pec-minor maintenance is daily, desk work re-tightens it.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W11 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep block on Tue covers the equivalent.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W11 flow.** **Today: do the PULL add-on** — dead hang 25s + scapular pulls x10. Do at the gym before pull-ups if no bar at home.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W11 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep block on Thu covers the equivalent (and the diagonal-reach band move is today's direct windmill prime).

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W11 flow.** **Today: do the LEGS add-on** — 90s pigeon (each side) + supine spinal twist. The W8 QL-spasm protocol stays non-negotiable on legs day. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above iliac crest beforehand if available.

activity.name: Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Flat or rolling trails. QL has been quiet at light loads; modest descent ok if it stays quiet.

tips: FORM-FIRST WEEK 2 — spotlights rotate, loads HOLD where W10 found edge-of-form. The cue is the progression.
Push spotlight: chest press machine (drill seat alignment + scap pack + 3s eccentric at 165). Layout shifts to station-focused.
Pull spotlights: chest-supported DB row + lat pulldown (drill chest-glued-to-bench + lats-to-back-pockets). Station-focused per the W10D3 meta-feedback.
Legs spotlight: leg press (drill foot mechanics + medium-ROM discipline). Squat HOLDS at 65 — brace pattern is locking in.
**Seated leg curl HARD RESET to 50** — W10D5 found prescribed 100 was a wrong-lever weight. Pad UNDER ANKLES. Rebuild from 50.
**Calf raise tempo shift** — double-leg slow OR single-leg faster, not both (W10D5 discovery).
KB Tue spotlights: B-stance swing mechanics + bent press leverage. C&P ramps 40 → 45 per W10D2 projection.
KB Thu spotlight: windmill mechanics (the bent press's sibling lift — same lateral hinge family).
Lunges HOLD at 25 — W10D5 confirmed no QL spasm. Earn the way back to 30 over multiple clean weeks.
QL is still the watchpoint. Abort any lift that twinges.

weeks.label: Form-First Week 3 — Spotlights Rotate Again
weeks.description: Third week of the form-first cycle. Spotlights rotate per the tracker: lateral raise + Kinesis cable fly (push), face pull + reverse fly (pull), seated leg curl + calf raise (legs), windmill + cossack mobility (KB Tue — already exists in fb-w12-kb-full), TGU left-side checkpoints (KB Thu). **W11 takeaways baked in:** chest press fatigue protocol (3 sets vs 4, drop to 150 if form breaks); incline DB held at 40 unless right shoulder is warm + quiet; Kinesis fallback numbers locked from W11D1 (lat raise 3, tri ext 5, straight-arm pulldown 6); pull-ups stepped up to 6-8 after W11D3's clean 7/7/7/6; squat pushed to 75 after W11D5's 'QL caught up'; seated leg curl 90 at corrected ankle placement is the new baseline; calf raise plate-under-foot for deeper stretch; incline RDL holds 25 dead slow; lunges optional (second clean week before W13 spotlight). The naming correction from W11D5 (incline RDL not back extension) is permanent.

schedule.label: PUSH (form-first, lat raise + cable fly spotlight)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W12 flow** (variety on W11). 15 min bodyweight + 3-5 min day-specific add-on. **Today: do the PUSH add-on** — doorframe pec-minor stretch (LOW/MID/HIGH), prone Y raises, scap push-ups.

schedule.label: KB FULL (NEW Asgooch overhead flow + windmill/cossack spotlight)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W12 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep on Tue covers the equivalent.

schedule.label: PULL (form-first, face pull + reverse fly spotlight)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W12 flow.** **Today: do the PULL add-on** — dead hang 25s + scapular pulls x10.

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W12 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep on Thu covers the equivalent.

schedule.label: LEGS (form-first, leg curl + calf raise spotlight)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W12 flow.** **Today: do the LEGS add-on** — 90s pigeon (each side) + supine spinal twist. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above iliac crest beforehand if available.

activity.name: Bay Area Hike
activity.notes: Flat or rolling trails. QL has been quiet at light loads through W11; modest descent ok if it stays quiet.

tips: FORM-FIRST WEEK 3 — spotlights rotate again. Loads HOLD where W11 found edge-of-form, push only where W11 actuals showed clean headroom.

**NEW (W12D1 discussion):** end-of-session 5-min cool-down block added to gym days starting W12D3, Sunday long-flow added as a permanent option on Day 7. Behind-back DB shrugs added as a closing block on pull days (rotates with trap-3 raises). See `docs/notes/functional-bulk/shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md` for full reasoning.
Push spotlights: lateral raise (drill scap-reset + slow eccentric at 15) + Kinesis cable fly (drill stretch + hug-a-tree at Kinesis 8).
Push fatigue test: chest press 3 sets at 165 (NOT 4), permission to drop to 150 if form breaks. No grinding 3-rep sets.
Pull spotlights: face pull (drill elbows-high + external rotation at 42.5) + reverse fly (lighter + cleaner at 20-22.5).
Pull-ups bumped to 6-8 after W11's clean 7/7/7/6 — mix grips: rack handles → flat bar wide → flat bar close → wide slow.
Legs spotlights: seated leg curl 90 at corrected ankle placement (the new baseline) + calf raise plate-under-foot for deeper stretch.
Squat pushes to 75/side after W11D5's 'QL caught up' signal — permission to drop to 70 if warmup is off.
Leg press: drill DEPTH at 3 plates (W11D5 went past medium ROM cleanly), NOT load. W13 tests 4 plates if depth at 3 holds.
Incline RDL HOLDS at 25lb dead slow — naming correction is permanent (not 'back extension').
Lunges optional this week (second clean QL week before W13 spotlight).

KB Tue: NEW @Asgooch overhead mobility complex (Row → Snatch → OH Lunge → OH Squat → Push Press) replaces dead-clean-press as Block 1 — genuinely fresh from the library, never integrated before. Spotlights stay: windmill + cossack mobility. Bent press rested this week (overhead pressing already loaded 4 ways in the new flow).
KB Thu spotlight: TGU left-side checkpoints. 1+1 per side per round, 3 rounds — fewer reps, cleaner reps.
QL is still the watchpoint. Abort any lift that twinges.

weeks.label: DELOAD — 3 Hard / 1 Deload (First Deload Since W9)
weeks.description: **DELOAD WEEK.** W10/W11/W12 were 3 hard weeks; W12 also added new lifts (behind-back shrugs, the cool-down block). The 3-hard/1-deload cadence — locked from W13 forward (next deloads W17, W21) — puts the cut here, and the fatigue signals back it up: the new right-shoulder symptom cluster (W12D1 — line-of-sensation + grip-triggered pain), universal tightness, multiple soft-tissue patterns. **HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70%** — drop a set per exercise. Spotlights still rotate (the cue work IS deload-appropriate): cable column block — lat raise + tri ext + straight-arm pulldown (push), bicep curl + high cable curl + cable crunch (pull), the NEW Bulgarian split squat (legs), C&P + TGU (KB Tue), bent press (KB Thu). **Three NEW elements introduced at deload volume:** farmer carry (push closing block), trap-3 raise (replaces the shrug block on pull this week), Bulgarian split squat (replaces the walking lunge permanently on legs). The connective tissue lags muscle adaptation 6-8 weeks — this week lets it catch up.

schedule.label: PUSH — DELOAD (cable column block spotlight + NEW farmer carry)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W13 flow** (rotated moves on W12; morning flows don't deload). **Today: do the PUSH add-on** — doorframe pec-minor stretch (LOW/MID/HIGH), prone Y raises, scap push-ups, + side-lying external rotation (worth it this week with the shoulder cluster active).

schedule.label: KB FULL — DELOAD (C&P + TGU spotlight, Asgooch flow cut to 3 rounds)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W13 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep on Tue covers the equivalent.

schedule.label: PULL — DELOAD (curl + crunch spotlight, trap-3 raise replaces shrugs)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W13 flow.** **Today: do the PULL add-on** — dead hang 25s + scapular pulls x10.

schedule.label: REST / LIGHT MOBILITY (Thursday is now a rest day — KB moved to Saturday to protect legs day)
LEGS — DELOAD (NEW Bulgarian split squat spotlight, replaces walking lunge)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W13 flow.** **Today: do the LEGS add-on** — the NEW cross-leg lateral lean + 90s pigeon (each side) + supine spinal twist. Lacrosse-ball QL roll above the iliac crest beforehand if available.

schedule.label: KB CONDITIONING — DELOAD (moved from Thursday — bent press spotlight + figure-8 flow)

flow.label: Morning Flow
flow.notes: **Universal W13 flow.** **Skip the day-specific add-on** — the in-workout banded prep covers the equivalent.

tips: DELOAD WEEK — first deload since W9. HOLD all loads, CUT volume ~60-70% (drop a set per exercise). The training effect is recovery + cue quality, not load. If a lift feels easy, that's the design.
**3 hard / 1 deload cadence is locked from W13 forward** — next deloads W17, W21.

**Schedule change — KB conditioning moves Thursday → Saturday; Thursday becomes rest.** W12D4's Thursday KB wrecked Friday legs (the QL came in tight). A rest day before legs + the KB day landing before Sunday rest fixes the collision. Testing the slot during this light week.

**Right shoulder symptom cluster is the headline watchpoint.** Cross-check W13D1 push vs. W13D3 pull — if it's worse, the hypothesis flips from functional to structural and a PT eval becomes priority.
Push: 2 sets chest press at 165, 2 sets incline DB at 45, cable column block spotlight (2 rounds). NEW farmer carry closing block (2 walks, 40-50lb).
Pull: pull-ups 3 sets, DB row 2 sets at 70 only (skip 60 and 80), curl/crunch spotlight. Trap-3 raise replaces the behind-back shrug block this week (planned rotation).
Legs: squat holds 75/side as a 3-set pyramid (8/6/8), leg press holds 3 plates / full ROM. NEW Bulgarian split squat replaces the walking lunge permanently — introduced form-only at deload volume.
KB Tue: Asgooch flow cut to 3 rounds per side, C&P + TGU spotlight refresh, C&P holds 45 (no 50).
KB Thu: bent press spotlight refresh, figure-8 flow stays — but the PRESCRIBED 2 rounds only (W12D4's bonus rounds tightened the QL before legs day).
**NEW QL/psoas release move** — the standing cross-leg lateral lean (W12D5 user discovery) is now in the legs warmup, the legs cool-down, and the legs-day morning add-on.
Cool-down stays — 5-min block, permanent every gym day. On a deload it's the connective-tissue catch-up window.
Sunday long flow encouraged this week — it fits the deload's recovery intent.
Gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) are for building size — go heavy, rest 2-3 min between sets
KB days are for conditioning and fun — lighter weight, keep moving, build work capacity
The 12-min run to/from the full gym = 72 min/week of Zone 2 for free
Day 6 is truly optional — rest if you're beat, do cardio if you're fired up
For the Functional Bulk goal: eat in a slight surplus, prioritize protein, sleep 7-8 hours
Each week has fixed exercises — no randomization — so you can track and beat your numbers
New weeks will be added over time with different exercises, rep schemes, and progressions
restGuidelines: Gym days: 2-3 min between compound sets, 60-90s between isolation. KB days: minimal rest within circuits, 45-60s between rounds. Zone 2: keep HR 120-140bpm.

overview.duration: Multi-week (ongoing — new weeks added over time)
overview.goal: Get functionally large — build size and strength in the gym while maintaining conditioning and athleticism. Unlike the standard version, this tracks your exact exercises week-to-week so you can measure progress.
overview.whoIsItFor: Intermediate lifters who want structure AND progression. You want to know exactly what you're doing each day, track your weights, and see yourself getting stronger week to week.
overview.whatToExpect: 3 heavy gym days (Push/Pull/Legs) for building size, 2 kettlebell days for conditioning, plus an optional Zone 2 day. Each week has a specific focus and fixed exercises. Week 1 establishes your baseline; future weeks introduce new exercises, higher volume, or different rep schemes.
keyPrinciples: Fixed exercises each week — no randomization — so you can track progression
Gym days are for building size — go heavy, rest 2-3 min between sets
KB days are for conditioning — lighter weight, keep moving
Each week has a different focus that builds on the previous
Eat in a slight surplus, prioritize protein, sleep 7-8 hours
Do 10 min daily mobility every morning — it won't affect recovery
overview.cycleNote: This is a multi-week program. Each week has its own set of workouts. Complete all sessions in a week before moving to the next. New weeks are developed over time based on your progress and goals.
overview.weeklyStructure: Mon: Push (Gym) → Tue: KB Full Body → Wed: Pull (Gym) → Thu: KB Conditioning → Fri: Legs (Gym) → Sat: Zone 2/Optional → Sun: Rest
